bids.reasoning: At t=0.0 with no opponent offers or messages yet, I target my maximum-utility opening bid. Since there is no time pressure at the start, I hold firm on every issue, especially the high-weight issues C and D.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid this early, conceding only on my lowest-weight issueA while holding firm on the more important issueB, issueC, and issueD. The opponent’s first offer is valid but gives me very low utility, and their message is an appeal rather than evidence of real concession, so at t=0.02 there is no need to move far yet.

I am still targeting a high-utility bid because it is very early (t=0.04) and the opponent has not conceded at all, repeating the all-valueA offer that is extremely poor for me. Their messages emphasize need and partnership, but the actual offers show no movement, so I will make only a small concession on my lowest-weight issueA while holding firm on the high-weight issues C and D and important issueB.

I am targeting a high-utility offer because it is still very early at t=0.06 and the opponent has not conceded at all, repeating the all-valueA bid that is worst for me. Their messages emphasize partnership and need, but the actual offers show no movement, so I will only make a small concession on my lowest-weight issue while holding firm on the high-weight issues C and D.

I am still targeting a high-utility bid because it is very early at t=0.08 and the opponent has not conceded at all, repeatedly offering all valueA, which is very poor for me. Their messages are emotional appeals, but the offer history shows no movement, so I will make only a small concession on my lowest-weight issue while holding firm on the high-weight issues.

I am targeting a high-utility early-game bid, conceding only on my lowest-weight issue while preserving issueB, issueC, and issueD where my utility stakes are much larger. The opponent has repeated the same all-valueA offer without numerical concession, and their messages are emotional appeals rather than evidence of movement, so at t=0.10 there is no reason to make a large concession yet.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.76 for me, since it is still early at t=0.12 and the opponent has only made a small numeric concession from all valueA to issueC:valueB. I have already conceded fully on low-weight issueA, and now make a modest concession on issueB while holding firm on the most important issues, issueC and issueD; the opponent’s partnership appeals are noted but not treated as binding evidence.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.73 because it is early in the negotiation and the opponent has only made a small concession on issueC while holding the other issues at their worst values for me. I have already conceded fully on low-weight issueA, so I will make a further moderate concession on issueB while holding firm on high-weight issueC and issueD. The opponent’s messages are emotional appeals, but their actual offers remain very demanding, so I should not over-concede yet.

I am targeting a high utility around 0.72 because it is still early at t=0.16 and the opponent has barely conceded, only moving issueC from valueA to valueB while holding the rest at valueA. I have already conceded fully on low-weight issueA and am making a small additional concession on issueB, while protecting high-weight issueC and issueD. The opponent’s partnership/emotional messages do not add binding evidence beyond their offers, which remain very low for my utility.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.72 for me, since it is still early at t=0.18 and there is no need to make large concessions yet. The opponent’s actual offers remain very demanding, with only a small movement on issueC from valueA to valueB, while their messages are mostly emotional appeals rather than reliable constraints. I will make a small non-repeating concession on the lower-weight issueB while holding firm on the high-weight issues C and D.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.66 by conceding fully on issueA and issueB while holding firm on the most important issues, issueC and issueD. At t=0.20 it is still early, so I should not move close to the opponent’s low offers yet; their actual bids show only a small concession on issueC, while the partnership language is cheap talk rather than binding evidence.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.64 because it is early enough not to concede heavily, but I should avoid repeating my last exact bid and show some movement. The opponent’s offers remain very low for me and their messages are mostly emotional appeals, but they have occasionally moved on issueC, so I will concede there slightly while holding firm on high-value issueD.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.62 by holding the most important remaining issueD near the top while keeping concessions on lower-value issues A and B and matching the opponent’s recent movement on issueC. Since time is only t=0.24, there is no need to collapse to the opponent’s low-utility bid, but I will make a small concession from issueD:valueF to issueD:valueE to signal progress. The opponent’s messages are emotional appeals, while their actual offers show only limited concession, mostly on issueC and once on issueA.

I am targeting a utility around 0.69 by keeping strong positions on the high-weight issues C and D while using the opponent’s latest concession on issueB. Since we are only at t=0.26, there is still time and the opponent has mostly held near valueA, so I should not concede too aggressively despite their repeated partnership appeals. Their messages are emotional but not binding; the actual offers show only small, inconsistent movement, especially no movement on issueD.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around the upper-mid range because it is only t=0.28 and the opponent’s offers remain quite low for me despite small concessions. Their bids suggest they may value lower values on several issues, but the main reliable signal is limited movement, so I am conceding on lower-weight issueB while holding firmer on the high-weight issueC and issueD.

I am targeting a utility around 0.69, which is still protective of my high-weight issues C and D while recognizing we are now in the early mid-game. The opponent’s messages are mostly emotional appeals, so I rely on the offers: they have begun making small concessions on individual issues, including their latest move to issueB: valueD. I will use that signal by accepting movement on B while holding firm on C and D, where my utility is much more important.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around the midgame by holding issueC at valueC and issueD at valueD while conceding low-value issueA fully. The opponent’s offers remain low for me overall, but they have signaled willingness on issueB=valueD, issueC=valueC, and most recently issueD=valueD, so I am packaging those concessions into a more realistic counter without relying on their emotional messages.

I am targeting a utility around 0.65 because we are still only at t=0.34 and the opponent’s offers remain very low-value for me despite their cooperative language. Their latest bid moved to issueA:valueB and issueC:valueB, but overall they are still holding firm on low values, so I will make only a small adjustment that acknowledges their signal on low-weight issueA while protecting my higher-weight issueC and issueD.

I targeted a utility around 0.64, making only a modest concession because we are still before the midpoint and the opponent’s offers remain much lower for me despite gradual movement. Their latest bid signals interest in issueB:valueC and prior willingness to move on issueD, so I concede on lower-weight issueA and align issueB while holding firmer on higher-weight issueC and issueD.

I am targeting about 0.65 utility, which is still reasonable at t=0.38 because we are not yet near the deadline but should keep moving toward combinations the opponent has actually signaled. The opponent’s messages are mostly emotional partnership appeals, so I rely on the offer history: they have separately shown willingness on issueA=valueB, issueB=valueC, issueC=valueC, and issueD=valueD, while my bid avoids repeating my last exact offer.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.60, conceding meaningfully from my prior bid while preserving value on the high-importance issues. The opponent’s actual offers show only modest concessions and their messages are mostly emotional appeals, so I am trusting the bid history: they have separately signaled willingness on issueA=valueB, issueB=valueC, issueC=valueD, and issueD=valueD.

I am targeting a utility around 0.60, which is a meaningful concession from my earlier offers but still protects the high-weight issues, especially issueD and issueC. The opponent has gradually improved from all valueA to offers including issueC:valueD and issueD:valueC, so I will match their movement on issueC and concede issueA fully while holding issueD at valueD. Their messages emphasize partnership and urgency, but the actual bidding history shows slow, incremental concessions rather than a firm constraint.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.58 by conceding on the lower-weight issues A and B while protecting the higher-weight issues C and D. At t=0.44 there is still time, but the opponent’s offers have slowly improved for me, especially around issueC and issueD, so I am making a meaningful concession without dropping to their latest low-utility bid. Their messages are cooperative but emotional, so I rely mainly on the offer pattern rather than the stated urgency.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around the high-0.5 range, conceding on lower-weight issueA and partially on issueB/issueD while still protecting the high-weight issueC. The opponent’s bids remain low for me but show real concessions across issueB, issueC, and issueD at different times; their emotional messages are cheap talk, so I rely mainly on the offer pattern. This bid avoids repeating my previous exact offer and combines values the opponent has already signaled at least some willingness to consider.

I am targeting a weighted utility around 0.61, which is a meaningful concession from my early maximum while still protecting the high-weight issueD and issueC positions. At t=0.48 we are mid-negotiation rather than at the deadline, so I should not collapse to the opponent’s latest low-utility offer; instead I combine concessions they have already individually signaled, especially issueA=valueB, issueB=valueB, issueC=valueE, and issueD=valueD. Their messages are mostly emotional partnership appeals, so I rely on the offer history rather than treating those claims as binding.

I am targeting a utility of about 0.60, which is a moderate mid-game concession while still protecting the high-weight issues C and D. The opponent’s messages are mostly emotional appeals, so I rely on the bids: they have shown willingness to move on issueB and issueD recently, and previously offered issueC: valueE, so I combine those signals into a plausible compromise without dropping to their current low-C demand.

I am targeting about 0.60 utility: enough concession for mid-negotiation, but still protecting my highest-weight issues, especially issueD and issueC. The opponent’s offers have gradually improved on some issues, but their latest still gives me low value on issueD; their messages are mostly emotional partnership appeals, so I rely on the bid history rather than the claims. This bid uses several values they have recently signaled while holding issueD at a strong but not maximal value.

I am targeting about 0.64 utility by accepting the opponent’s latest concessions on issueB and issueD while still protecting issueC, one of my highest-weight issues. At t=0.54 there is meaningful time pressure but not enough to accept their low-utility offer; their offers show gradual movement, especially recently on issueD, while the emotional messages are not binding.

I am targeting a moderate utility bid around 0.62, conceding on lower-weight issueB while preserving strong value on high-weight issueD and acceptable value on issueC. At t=0.56 there is meaningful time pressure but not a deadline crisis, so I am combining values the opponent has actually offered recently or previously rather than trusting their repeated emotional partnership appeals. Their latest bid signals willingness on issueA=valueD and issueC=valueD, and they previously offered issueD=valueE, so this is a plausible tradeoff without dropping too far.

I am targeting a utility around 0.64, which is a moderate concession at t=0.58 while still protecting my higher-weight issues, especially issueC and issueD. The opponent’s messages are mostly emotional appeals, so I rely on the offer history: their latest bid made a meaningful move on issueB to valueF and issueD to valueC, while still holding issueC very low. I will accept their recent movement on issueB and issueD but counter with issueC at valueD to avoid dropping too far on a high-importance issue.

I am targeting a utility around 0.59, only modestly above the opponent’s latest offer to me, because at t=0.60 we should start closing the remaining gap while preserving value on my high-weight issues. The opponent’s bids have gradually improved for me, especially on issueD, and their latest offer already gave me issueD: valueE, so I am keeping that while asking for a manageable improvement on issueC. Their messages are cooperative but repetitive cheap talk, so I rely mainly on the actual bid movement.

I am targeting a solid mid-late utility around 0.65 while moving on the opponent-signaled areas rather than accepting their latest low-utility package. The opponent’s messages are mostly emotional appeals, so I rely on the offer history: they have shown willingness on issueB=valueF and sometimes issueD=valueC, while issueC=valueD has appeared in their concessions, so this bid combines those signals while protecting my high-weight issues.

I am targeting a utility around 0.60, conceding meaningfully from my prior position while preserving value on the high-weight issues, especially issueC and some minimum movement on issueD. The opponent’s bids have improved for me over time, with the latest offer conceding issueC to valueF and issueB to valueD, but they are still holding issueD very low; their messages are emotional partnership appeals, so I rely mainly on the actual bid history. At t=0.64, I will accept their latest movement on issueA, issueB, and issueC, while asking for issueD at valueC, which they have previously signaled as possible.

I am targeting a modest improvement over the opponent’s latest offer, which already gives me about 0.60 utility, by asking for issueC to move from valueB to valueD while keeping their recent concessions on issueB and issueD. At t=0.66 there is meaningful time pressure, so I am not reverting to high demands, but I still want to preserve value on the high-weight issueC. The opponent’s messages are emotional/partnership appeals, but the actual offers show recent willingness to concede on issueB and issueD, with some prior movement on issueC.

I am targeting a utility around 0.62, making a measured concession at t=0.68 without dropping to the opponent’s full last offer. The opponent’s messages are mostly emotional appeals, so I rely on the offers: their bids have improved but remain erratic, and their latest offer already gave issueA=valueB, issueC=valueD, issueD=valueB while they have previously shown willingness on issueB=valueF. This bid keeps three elements from their latest offer and asks them to return to valueF on issueB.

I targeted a solid late-mid utility around 0.68 by keeping the opponent’s latest concessions on issueB=valueF and issueC=valueC while asking for a modest improvement on issueD. At t=0.70 there is meaningful time pressure, but the opponent has repeatedly shown willingness to offer issueD=valueC, so this is a realistic counter rather than a hardline demand; their emotional messages are noted but I rely on the actual offer pattern.
